prompt:
Two production complaints about dashfeed, our little dashboard edge service, and I've stared at the code long enough that it all looks fine to me.

1) Whenever a deploy drains a pod, some client that was mid-download on /report/... gets a response the monitoring proxy flags as 'trailing data after JSON document' — support forwarded me one capture and it's literally our timeout error with what looks like half a report smashed onto the end of it. Clients that hit a healthy pod are fine.

2) The stats-backend team opened a ticket: when their service has a bad minute and answers 500s, every /summary poll from us shows up on a brand-new TCP connection (their conn table fills up with our pods), and shortly after that our own /summary stops answering at all until someone restarts the pod. When their backend is healthy nobody sees anything weird.

Someone on the platform team turned both reports into deterministic repros — the last two tests in dashfeed_test.go. The four tests above them pass and describe behavior that must keep working. Find whatever is wrong in the service and fix it; dashfeed_test.go is off limits. Done means go test -race -timeout 30s ./... is green.
